 {"id":"git_safety_guard-53q","title":"E2E: explain/simulate/scan CLI flows","description":"## Objective

Add E2E coverage for non-hook CLI flows to ensure parity and stability.

## Scope

- `dcg explain` outputs (pretty/json/compact) are structurally valid.
- `dcg simulate` input formats are parsed end-to-end with realistic fixtures.
- `dcg scan` modes (`--staged`, explicit paths, JSON output) behave with expected exit codes.

## Logging

- On failure, dump command output + a minimal context snippet.

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E runs in CI without external dependencies.
- Format outputs are validated with jq or schema checks.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz","title":"Auto-Suggested Allowlists from History","description":"# Epic: Auto-Suggested Allowlists from History

## Priority Score: 700/1000 (Third Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Manually writing allowlist patterns is tedious and error-prone. Users either give up and disable DCG, write overly broad patterns, or spend hours crafting precise patterns.

This feature analyzes the history database to identify commands that are repeatedly blocked but appear safe, then generates suggested allowlist entries with confidence scoring for user approval.

## Example Workflow

User runs: dcg suggest-allowlist

Output shows:
  HIGH CONFIDENCE (blocked 127 times, consistent pattern):
    Commands: npm run build, npm run test, npm run lint
    Suggested pattern: npm run (build|test|lint)
    Risk assessment: LOW (read-only npm scripts)
    Accept? [y/N]

## Technical Approach

1. Query history for denied commands grouped by pattern_triggered
2. Cluster similar commands using normalized edit distance
3. Generate regex patterns that match each cluster
4. Score confidence based on frequency, consistency, risk factors
5. Present interactive CLI for approval/rejection
6. On accept, append to user allowlist config

## Clustering Algorithm

- Normalize all commands
- Tokenize into (command, subcommand, flags, args)
- Group by (command, subcommand, flags)
- For each group, find common argument patterns
- Generate regex from pattern

## Risk Scoring Factors

- Command frequency (more = higher confidence)
- Argument consistency (similar args = higher confidence)
- Dangerous flags present (-f, --force = lower confidence)
- Path sensitivity (system paths = lower confidence)

## Key Files

- src/history/schema.rs - Ensure rule_id tracking
- New: src/suggest.rs - Clustering and pattern generation
- src/cli.rs - Add dcg suggest-allowlist command
- src/allowlist.rs - Config writing integration

## Success Criteria

- Suggestions generated from at least 10 denied commands
- Confidence tiers: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW with clear criteria
- Interactive approval with risk explanation
- Generated patterns are valid regex
- Config file updated atomically

## Dependencies

Requires rule_id tracking in history (see Rule Metrics epic)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:34.569738697-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:34.569738697-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.1","title":"[Suggest] Risk scoring + explicit manual confirmation","description":"# Task: Risk scoring + explicit manual confirmation

## Purpose
Suggestions must never auto-write allowlist entries. Each suggestion should include
an explicit risk score and require manual confirmation before writing.

## Requirements
- Risk scoring independent from confidence scoring
- Risk categories: low / medium / high (deterministic rules)
- Interactive CLI must require explicit confirmation per entry
- Non-interactive mode outputs suggestions only (no writes)

## Implementation
- Add `risk_score` to suggestion model
- Define scoring rules (flags like `--force`, destructive verbs, system paths)
- Enforce confirmation in CLI flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- No allowlist entry is written without a user confirmation step
- Risk score is visible in both text and JSON outputs

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.2","title":"[Suggest] Audit logging for suggested allowlist entries","description":"# Task: Audit logging for suggested allowlist entries

## Purpose
Ensure every accepted suggestion is recorded for traceability and review.

## Requirements
- Log accepted suggestion to history with: rule_id (if any), pattern, risk score,
  user action (accepted/modified), and timestamp
- Include source cluster stats (frequency, sample commands)

## Implementation
- Extend history schema for suggestion events or reuse existing logging channel
- Update suggest-allowlist CLI to emit audit entries on accept

## Acceptance Criteria
- Accepting a suggestion writes an audit record
- Non-interactive mode produces **no writes**

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.3","title":"[Suggest] Unit tests for clustering + pattern + risk scoring","description":"# Task: Unit tests for clustering + pattern + risk scoring

## Purpose
Ensure core suggestion algorithms behave deterministically and safely.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for clustering (grouping correctness)
- Unit tests for pattern generation (anchoring, no overbroad matches)
- Unit tests for risk scoring rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests include negative cases (unsafe commands not generalized)
- Output is deterministic across runs

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.4","title":"[Suggest] E2E tests for suggest-allowlist CLI + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for suggest-allowlist CLI + verbose logs

## Purpose
Validate end-to-end behavior of suggest-allowlist, including interactive and
non-interactive output paths, with detailed logs on failure.

## Requirements
- Fixture history DB or log with known denied commands
- Tests for JSON output (non-interactive)
- Tests for interactive flow (accept/skip) using scripted input
- Ensure no allowlist writes occur in non-interactive mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests pass with clear, verbose failure logs
- CLI behavior matches documented contract

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.5","title":"[Suggest] Unit tests for audit logging + allowlist write safety","description":"# Task: Unit tests for audit logging + allowlist write safety

## Purpose
Ensure accepted suggestions are audited and that non-interactive mode performs
no writes.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for audit log emission on accept
- Tests ensuring non-interactive mode does not write allowlist or audit entries
- Tests for modified pattern flow (accept after edit)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Audit records include risk score, pattern, and frequency metadata
- Safety guarantees are enforced by tests

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.1","title":"[Cross] Config toggles for highlighting + explanations","description":"# Task: Config toggles for highlighting + explanations

## Purpose
Allow users to enable/disable new UX output (highlighting, explanations)
without recompiling or forking.

## Requirements
- Config keys with defaults that preserve existing behavior
- Hook output respects toggles
- CLI output respects toggles

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config can independently toggle highlighting and explanations
- Default behavior remains safe and predictable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.2","title":"[Cross] Explain JSON schema update + version bump","description":"# Task: Explain JSON schema update + version bump

## Purpose
If new fields are added to explain output (highlight spans, explanations),
update the JSON schema version and documentation.

## Requirements
- Bump `EXPLAIN_JSON_SCHEMA_VERSION`
- Update JSON output struct docs and README
- Ensure backward compatibility notes are explicit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema version bump recorded in docs
- `dcg explain --format json` includes new fields with stable names

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.3","title":"[Cross] Performance budget checks for new steps","description":"# Task: Performance budget checks for new steps

## Purpose
Ensure new formatting and explanation logic does not regress hook performance.

## Requirements
- Identify new steps that should be timed (highlight formatting, explanation lookup)
- Add budget thresholds or confirm existing budgets are sufficient
- Add perf tests or benchmarks if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- No measurable regression vs existing budget targets
- Budget expectations documented if new steps are added

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.5","title":"[Cross] Unit tests for config toggles (highlighting/explanations)","description":"# Task: Unit tests for config toggles (highlighting/explanations)

## Purpose
Verify that config toggles reliably enable/disable highlighting and explanations
without affecting decision logic.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for config parsing + defaults
- Tests that toggles suppress/enable output sections
- Ensure toggles do not change allow/deny decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both toggles independently and together
- Failures show expected vs actual output sections

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.6","title":"[Cross] E2E tests for config toggles + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for config toggles + verbose logs

## Purpose
Validate end-to-end behavior when highlighting/explanations are toggled on/off
via config, with verbose logging on failure.

## Requirements
- Fixture config files for each toggle combination
- E2E test that runs hook/CLI and asserts output sections
- Log full stdout/stderr on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests confirm toggles work without affecting decisions
- Logs are sufficient to debug failures

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.7","title":"[Cross] Perf regression coverage for highlight/explain output","description":"# Task: Perf regression coverage for highlight/explain output

## Purpose
Ensure newly added formatting/explanation logic does not regress hot-path
performance (hook mode).

## Requirements
- Add/extend perf benchmarks to include highlight + explanation output paths
- Capture timings with detailed logs on regression
- Tie to existing perf budgets in `src/perf.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
